overview: The Lands deck is a Legacy archetype that abuses the format's extensive land pool to generate mana, control the board, and win through specific combos. It relies on an engine of Mox Diamond, Exploration, Crop Rotation, and Life from the Loam to cycle lands and recover card advantage. Recently, the deck has integrated four Sphere of Resistance into the main deck to improve consistency against faster strategies.
win_conditions: Deploying Marit Lage to finish games faster
Executing the Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage finishing combo
Overwhelming opponents with an avalanche of land resources via Life from the Loam

keep_criteria: Hands that can play Forest - Exploration - Saga on turn 1
Hands threatening Marit Lage by turn 2 against decks without counters
Risky hands that can buy time and likely draw a green mana source soon
Keep hands with 4-5 lands (especially fetches, duals, or signets), Mox Diamond, Life from the Loam, Exploration, or early threats like Tectonic Giant/Thrun.
Keep any hand with Mox Diamond + fetch land, or Loam + 2 lands.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with no action or acceleration against almost everything
Hands relying on Marit Lage when facing decks with counterspells
Mulligan to hands that can play a land or rock on turn 1, or dig for consistency.

examples.reason: It doesn't have acceleration, nor any action. Verdict: Mulligan against almost everything.

You can both rely on drawing one of your many lands and play the sequence of Forest - Exploration - Saga on turn 1, Crop Rotation into a fetchland, tap it, and Life from the Loam to recover both lands and play the Sphere.
Risky keep; a bet on drawing a green mana source. It can buy you some time.
Threatens to play Marit Lage at the end of turn 2. Keep against decks with no answer, mulligan against decks with counters.

early_game: Play lands to establish a robust mana base
Hold off attackers with Maze of Ith, The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale, and Karakas
Deploy Sphere of Resistance to slow down opponent's spell casting
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Mox Diamond if possible, or fetch a dual.
Turn 2: Cast Life from the Loam to dig 2-3 cards, play a second land, then cast another mana rock or Exploration.
Sequence: Fetch -> Land -> Dig/Rock -> Land -> Rock/Exploration.
Prioritize acceleration over threats until turn 3-4.
mid_game: Control opponent's mana with Wasteland and Ghost Quarter
Utilize Urza's Saga as a toolbox to find answers like Haywire Mite or Soul-Guide Lantern
Cycle lands with Crop Rotation and recover them with Life from the Loam
Prioritize mana acceleration and digging over card draw.
Use Life from the Loam to dig 2-3 cards, but avoid digging into lands when you need threats.
Manage your graveyard carefully against hate, and sequence mana rocks to ensure you can cast Exploration or threats when needed.
Hold back early threats until you have 5+ mana or a clear path to victory.
Commit when you can protect your board with multiple threats or have enough mana to answer removal.
late_game: Summon Marit Lage to secure victory
Execute the Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage combo
Overwhelm opponents with continuous land production and resource advantage
Tectonic Giant, Thrun, Breaker of Silence, or casting Exploration with 4+ lands in play to win on the spot.
Secure these by protecting them with multiple threats or using mana rocks to cast around removal.
Go all-in when you have 5+ mana, a threat on board, and enough pressure to close the game.
Commit to Exploration only if you have 4+ lands and a guaranteed win, or if the opponent has no answers.

key_cards.name: Mox Diamond
key_cards.notes: Converts lands into early mana to speed up the engine.

key_cards.name: Exploration
key_cards.notes: Allows tapping lands for extra mana to fuel the combo.

key_cards.name: Crop Rotation
key_cards.notes: Fetches specific lands like Karakas or fetchlands to enable combos and control.

key_cards.name: Life from the Loam
key_cards.notes: Returns lands from the graveyard to hand, recovering card disadvantage.

key_cards.name: Sphere of Resistance
key_cards.notes: Slows down combo decks by increasing mana cost of spells.

key_cards.name: Dark Depths
key_cards.notes: Part of the finishing combo with Thespian's Stage.

key_cards.name: Thespian's Stage
key_cards.notes: Copies Dark Depths to create a massive creature.

key_cards.name: Marit Lage
key_cards.notes: Large creature that helps finish games faster.

key_cards.name: Urza's Saga
key_cards.notes: Generates mana and exiles cards to find sideboard options like Haywire Mite.

key_cards.name: Wasteland
key_cards.notes: Destroys opponent's lands to control their mana.

sequencing_tips: Play Forest, then Exploration, then Urza's Saga on turn 1 to accelerate land drops.
Crop Rotation into a fetchland, tap it for mana, then cast Life from the Loam to recover both lands and play Sphere of Resistance.
Prioritize holding off attackers with Maze of Ith or Karakas before committing to the combo.
Use Urza's Saga to exile and retrieve sideboard cards like Haywire Mite or Soul-Guide Lantern as needed.

Common mistakes include overcommitting to Exploration without a finisher, mismanaging Life from the Loam by digging into lands when you need threats, and failing to mulligan aggressively for early plays.

Unique interactions: Mox Diamond can be sacrificed for 2 mana, Life from the Loam can fetch any land, and Exploration can be cast from the graveyard if supported by effects like Reanimate or Undying Evil (though rare in Modern).
Always track the opponent's graveyard and mana base to predict their answers.

matchups.advice: Remove Spheres and some Saga targets. Rely on the Life from the Loam engine to overwhelm them with resources, as they have limited Wastelands and Forces of Will.
Use main deck answers like Bojuka Bog, Karakas, and Maze of Ith. Swords to Plowshares is crucial for Archon of Cruelty. Spheres are less effective here.
Recruit Marit Lage as soon as possible before Initiative resolves. Use Dark Depths to potentially remove Magus of the Moon. Sideboard Torpor Orb and Deafening Silence.
Battle for resources. Main deck can handle Uro. Sphere can force out threats like Choke. Marit Lage is a backup win condition.
Use Sphere of Resistance to delay them. Torpor Orb from the sideboard can be an ace in the hole. Aim to finish with Marit Lage.

Cast multiple low-cost threats to force counter trade-offs, use Life from the Loam to dig for answers, and sequence mana rocks to cast around counters. If possible, cast Exploration in response to counterspells or use it from the graveyard if supported.

Out-ramp them with Life from the Loam and Exploration. Do not trade creatures; focus on generating more mana and card advantage to win the late game. Use Thrun or Breaker of Silence to protect your board.

Mulligan aggressively for early threats like Tectonic Giant or Thrun. Use Life from the Loam to dig for answers and stabilize. Apply early pressure to force the opponent to answer you, then close the game with a massive threat.

Apply consistent pressure with threats and use Life from the Loam to out-value their draw spells. Win by generating more card advantage and mana than they can answer. Protect your threats with multiple board presence or removal.

It is a Life from the Loam and Exploration race. Win by having better sequencing, more threats, and graveyard hate. Prioritize digging for threats over lands, and use Thrun or Breaker of Silence to protect your board while out-valuing your opponent.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, discard, and graveyard hate.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: This deck's base exists since Legacy was created: Mox Diamond , Exploration , Crop Rotation and Life from the Loam are useful to abuse the extensive land options available in this format.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: The possibility of summoning Marit Lage helped this deck finish games faster
we added the Dark Depths Thespian's Stage finishing combo
the Life from the Loam engine will, sooner or later, bury them under an avalanche of resources

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: You can both rely on drawing one of your many lands and play the sequence of Forest - Exploration - Saga on turn 1, Crop Rotation into a fetchland, tap it, and Life from the Loam to recover both lands and play the Sphere.
hold off attackers with Maze of Ith , The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale and Karakas

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: For years, this deck stood on a delicate balance between having positive games against the several Delver versions, and breaking a sweat to deal with combo decks.
Blood Moon / Magus of the Moon / Archon of Emeria are the cards you definitely don't want to see in front of you.
The problem is Archon of Cruelty , so your Swords to Plowshares shine here.
Force of Negation is useful to deal with Life from the Loam
